title: Chaetaria oligantha
taxonomy: Chaetaria oligantha is classified as a genus and species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Chloridoideae. The genus Chaetaria is further classified under the tribe Cynodonteae. The species Chaetaria oligantha is native to tropical regions of Africa. This classification indicates its evolutionary relationships and positioning within the broader plant kingdom.
uses:
Chaetaria oligantha is a plant species that has been found to have several medicinal properties. However, specific information on its medicinal uses is limited due to the lack of comprehensive research. 

Chaetaria oligantha is traditionally used in some parts of the world to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. Further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. The plant's medicinal properties are not yet widely recognized, and its use should be approached with caution.
